meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Master AI Debugging: Solve Complex Code Bugs Like a Pro
content.seo_description: Stop staring at broken code. Learn to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to debug complex issues, accelerate your development, and build better portfolios.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't about guessing; it's about systematic analysis. By integrating Antigravity for architectural visualization and Claude Code for iterative CLI-based patching, you transform opaque errors into actionable fix paths. This workflow allows you to diagnose logic flaws, memory leaks, and dependency conflicts in minutes rather than hours.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the cursor blinks, and the console screams a cryptic stack trace that defies logic. You have spent hours chasing a ghost in the machine, and the frustration is mounting. Every developer has stood at this precipice, questioning their ability to ship a project that actually works.

But here is the secret: senior engineers don't debug by memorizing every error. They debug by using intelligent systems to surface the truth. You are about to bridge the gap between 'can't debug' and 'master builder' by mastering the modern stack of AI-assisted diagnostics.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Point
steps.description: Don't feed the entire repository to your AI. Use Antigravity to map the specific module causing the crash.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the attached error trace and identify the specific function responsible for the memory overflow.

steps.title: Contextualize with Claude Code
steps.description: Feed the stack trace and the relevant file snippet to Claude Code to generate a root-cause hypothesis.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain why this state mutation is failing in the current React lifecycle context.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Analyze this stack trace and identify the state mutation conflict in index.js'

steps.title: Simulate the Fix
steps.description: Run a dry-run of the proposed fix to ensure it doesn't introduce regressions in your codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a refactor that fixes the null pointer exception while maintaining the existing API contract.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply the proposed refactor to the user authentication module'

steps.title: Automate Validation
steps.description: Use your new AI tools to generate unit tests that verify the fix holds up under stress.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Jest test suite that covers the edge cases previously causing the crash.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a test file for the auth module and run the suite'

steps.title: Implement and Ship
steps.description: Finalize the code implementation and commit the changes to your repository with a clean history.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review this final diff for any lingering anti-patterns before I commit.
steps.claude_code_command: git commit -m 'fix: resolve runtime null pointer in auth module via AI diagnostic'

pro_tips: Always ask the AI to explain the 'why' behind the bug, not just the code fix, to build your own mental model.
Use Antigravity to visualize data flow; if you can see the state movement, you can spot the leak.
Keep your Claude Code session scoped to one feature at a time to prevent 'context fatigue' where the AI loses track of file logic.
When an AI suggests a fix, ask it to perform a 'devil's advocate' review to identify potential side effects.
Document your debugging process in your commit messages; it turns a 'fix' into a record of professional growth.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully patched your first bug, push your changes to GitHub and document the process in your portfolio. Then, pick a more complex module to optimize using the same workflow.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs define your limits. With the right tools, you aren't just a student; you are an AI-augmented developer ready to ship production-grade software. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
